id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-0
text: I had lived in Metro 112 for almost 4 years. I'm probably one of the tenants who stayed longest. I even witnessed many staff came and left, like Kimber, Judy, Kellie (another Kellie) and so on. I think it's time to get them a review after moving out.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-1
text:
I think it's time to get them a review after moving out. I started with things I really like:
1. Fantastic location
2. Friendly staff and responsive maintenance team
3. Helped receiving packages
4. Moving out process is smoother than I expected
5. They remember your Birthdays and will write you a card typically

Some perks:
1. Some gift cards randomly during holidays;
2. Pies send out in Thanksgiving;
3. Brunch / cocktail / other activities (like yoga) roughly once per year. Things I don't like:
1. The walls in between are incredibly thin (no insulation) and not sound proof at all.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-2
text: Our bedroom is next to our neighbor's living room and I can hear very clearly what they are watching when I was on my bed. We used to complain their late parties at night, which made me crazy and couldn't sleep. After complaint, it's getting better but they still like to have parties on Fri/Sat night. Sometimes I can hear what they talked about especially at quite night. It's very awkward to hear that as no privacy in the apartment. I guess our bathroom is next to their kitchen as well. I often hear they put something into the microwave and the "ding" sound when I sat on toilet.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-3
text: I hope their new apartments which is under construction will resolve this issue and put some insulation even in interior walls. 2. They don't care about you if it's something related to your neighbors. Our balcony floor constantly has nuts shells, seeds and corns etc. thrown from upper stairs. I noticed that they have two buckets hanging outside and some seeds dropped out from that two buckets. I assume our neighbors intend to feed the birds, but they are not aware of that the winds and birds keep the garbage out of buckets and dropped to our balcony.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-4
text: We reported this issue to Metro 112 more than 4 times but never get resolved. This lasted for several months and we gave up complaining to Metro 112 because like I said, they don't care. 3. Their pricing system is not reliable and make me doubt their price. Before we moved in 4 years ago, we really liked one specific room. But when we wanted sign contract one week after and were told that apartment had been rented out. Luckily they have another one with same floor plan exactly three floors right downstairs of previous one with a $100 higher rent.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-5
text: It was unacceptable to get a lower floor with higher price (one week difference, so no market change). They couldn't explain it and just said they ran the system and got price. They probably feel it was unfair either so waived our parking fees. One year later when we wanted to renew the contract, they increased the rent by a lot ~200+, which is ridiculous. And they didn't waive parking any more, so the total would be increased by $300. We tried to negotiate the price but they still said the price was given by system.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-6
text:
They refused to give any promotion to loyal customers who paid all the rent on time. Then we really worked hard to find other apartments and tried to move out. At the end we were so busy and ran out of time, so renewed with Metro 112.

The third year, they increased the rent by ~$200 again, we tried to negotiate the rent again of course. Out of surprise, it worked and our rent only increased by a really small amount. After that, I started to be suspicious about their pricing system. The fourth year, we renewed the contract month by month (or 3 months).
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-7
text:
The fourth year, we renewed the contract month by month (or 3 months). The monthly rent was even cheaper than the previous 12-months-contract monthly rent. Even the renting market cooled down recently, it's still unbelievable. 4. Smelly hallways sometimes (mainly caused by animal waste, some one who doesn't follow the rules smoke)

5. Garage door is always broken. As I said, I've lived here for almost 4 years and I couldn't count how many times the security door was broken. So they had to open the door and let all the cars get in and out.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt::chunk-8
text: So they had to open the door and let all the cars get in and out. The problems is many cars don't pay parking are still parking inside. They didn't tow them away even they claimed to do so. Overall, we are still happy with Metro 112 even I wrote many negative things. As I have friends lived in comparable apartments nearby in downtown Bellevue, I heard many much worse comments about those apartments. I suppose Metro 112 does a great job among competitors in general and I still recommend.
source_file: Metro 112 -  Vivian H. Bellevue, WA - Feb 22, 2018.txt

id: Metro 112 - Summer C. Bellevue, WA - Sep 28, 2019.txt::chunk-0
text: Lived here for two years now. It had an amazing staff when I first started. Adam and Kim are both extremely kind and helpful, and working with them have been a pleasure. However, Adam recently left and Kim has been promoted, so you end up having to deal with Brittany a lot of the times. Brittany, on the other hand, is not so friendly. Any questions you have for her seems to be a headache to her, and she just wants you to go away as soon as possible. The more time you spend in front of her desk seems to be directly proportional to how much stress you're causing her.
source_file: Metro 112 - Summer C. Bellevue, WA - Sep 28, 2019.txt

id: Metro 112 - Summer C. Bellevue, WA - Sep 28, 2019.txt::chunk-1
text: You spend a lot of time week to week interacting with the Metro 112 staff, and you used to feel like you're part of this big family, but it's feeling less and less so. I tend to avoid visiting the office if Brittany's the only one there, just because of how unpleasant the interaction with her tends to be. If Brittany's the one that lead you on your apartment tour, I would recommend doing another tour with Kim or Julia! This is an otherwise nice place to live.
source_file: Metro 112 - Summer C. Bellevue, WA - Sep 28, 2019.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon Lorraine I. Los Angeles, CA - Sep 4, 2021.txt::chunk-0
text:
I don't understand why people don't like here
Maybe because where their apt is located in the site, but the location of this building itself is unbeatable
For sure Avalon is a luxury apt and their price is straightly goes up every day. But I am facing the courtyard and I can never hear anything from my neighbor and it is super quiet which is extremely important to me. Go downstairs there are plenty of shops restaurants although some of them are just having beautiful price but not tasty, overall the location is super safe cuz this is Bellevue.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon Lorraine I. Los Angeles, CA - Sep 4, 2021.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon Lorraine I. Los Angeles, CA - Sep 4, 2021.txt::chunk-1
text: I am so happy here every day, downstairs is the downtown park of Bellevue I love walking there every morning and night.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon Lorraine I. Los Angeles, CA - Sep 4, 2021.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 154444 Resident • 2013 - Jun 13, 2013.txt::chunk-0
text: As others have said, Noisy building. I can hear my neighbors TV, people in the hall way, keys rattling.. etc.. On the bright side I can always find a parking spot and it feels very safe to walk around. Great location next to the mall and lot of restaurants near by. The best part is safeway downstairs. and the Gym is mostly empty at the times I've gone. Inside the apartment the paint is chipped, the materials used is cheap, the walls are thin, all appliances work as expected but don't expect luxury nice. I don't think there is AC in this building..
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 154444 Resident • 2013 - Jun 13, 2013.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 154444 Resident • 2013 - Jun 13, 2013.txt::chunk-1
text: I don't think there is AC in this building.. the One time I tried to turn it on all I got was heat. I have a small window that looks out into the courtyard. Great view but noisy if there are neighbors playing with their dogs in the courtyard. The doors are a bit tricky but once you learn the 3 main exit routes it is easier to get in and out. Elevators are super slow but they eventually turn up. For the price this place asks for and for what you are getting. I don't think it is worth it.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 154444 Resident • 2013 - Jun 13, 2013.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 392713 Resident • 2008 - 2011 - Jan 22, 2011.txt::chunk-0
text: My wife and I have lived here for over 2 years and are about to renew our lease for another year. Avalon Meydenbauer has been a great place live. Location is best as it is right in the heart of downtown Bellevue with the convenience of the downtown park, shopping, dining, entertainment, and cinemas all right across the street and, best of all, a Safeway with Starbucks right under us. The leasing office staff has completely changed since we first moved in and the new staff is always friendly and helpful, answering any and all questions we may have.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 392713 Resident • 2008 - 2011 - Jan 22, 2011.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 392713 Resident • 2008 - 2011 - Jan 22, 2011.txt::chunk-1
text: We have never had any problems with picking up packages that were delivered to the office. The maintenance staff is also very friendly, greeting us in the hallways, and they are always timely with our maintenance requests. The fitness center is spacious and very convenient. We like the individual TVs for the treadmills/ellipticals. Parking in the 2-level garage has never been a problem for us. We wish there were more visitor parking spaces, but I guess we ve been lucky with the few spaces whenever we do have visitors. The unit we live in suits us very well.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 392713 Resident • 2008 - 2011 - Jan 22, 2011.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 392713 Resident • 2008 - 2011 - Jan 22, 2011.txt::chunk-2
text: The unit we live in suits us very well. We are on the middle floor facing Safeway s front parking lot. We rarely ever hear our neighbors. Outside noise (sirens, grocery carts, etc.) is expected and doesn t bother us too much. The unit does get hot in the summer time so we thought a $300 portable air conditioner was a good investment. Overall, we are very pleased with Avalon Meydenbauer. With the awesome location and conveniences, it will be difficult for us to move out (when it comes time).
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, Current Resident 392713 Resident • 2008 - 2011 - Jan 22, 2011.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, livinginrent Resident • 2009-2010 - Dec, 22, 2010.txt::chunk-0
text: I have lived in this place for more than a year now and I love it! - Location, location, location. You just cannot find a better area right in the heart of Downtown Bellevue and on top of a Safeway. Living on top of Safeway is a big luxury, I drop by any time to buy milk, bread or anything else I need. - New construction. The building is new, the walls are thick, I have never heard a peep from my neighbors (But I also live on the topmost floor facing the courtyard, so it is super quiet). The amenities inside the house are good and the kitchen looks posh.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, livinginrent Resident • 2009-2010 - Dec, 22, 2010.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, livinginrent Resident • 2009-2010 - Dec, 22, 2010.txt::chunk-1
text: The amenities inside the house are good and the kitchen looks posh. It is peak winter now and I have turned on my heater just a couple of times. The windows are air tight and provide great insulation. - Friendly staff. The staff is very helpful and resolve any problems quickly. - Parking. I have never had trouble finding easy parking with a compact car. (except on late nights on weekdays) - Online facilities. Rent/maintenance etc. can all be handled online. - Package handling. The staff take care of any Fedex packages etc you receive and handle it well.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, livinginrent Resident • 2009-2010 - Dec, 22, 2010.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, livinginrent Resident • 2009-2010 - Dec, 22, 2010.txt::chunk-2
text: The staff take care of any Fedex packages etc you receive and handle it well. The only big negative in this place is visitor's parking. There is no way to open the garage door for your buddy sitting in your apartment. You have to go down to the garage, drive up to the garage entrance and open the door. I just ask my buddies to find parking outside closeby. Overall, a great place to live and you will enjoy it here!
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, livinginrent Resident • 2009-2010 - Dec, 22, 2010.txt

id: Meydenbauer Avalon, Pratik W. Seattle, WA - Dec 27, 2017.txt::chunk-0
text: Great apartments. Rooms are spacious. Staff is very friendly. All the maintenance request are responded within a day. Location is great. Everything you need is at a walkable distance. Its in the heart of downtown bellevue.Downtown bellevue park is right in front.the building also has a Safeway, which is an added advantage as you dont have to walk for groceries. Though they had some issue couple of years back, would definitely recommend it.
source_file: Meydenbauer Avalon, Pratik W. Seattle, WA - Dec 27, 2017.txt

id: Surrey on the Main - Utopi D. San Francisco, CA - Apr 13, 2026.txt::chunk-0
text: Aside from amenities/location-- there is major miscommunication regarding timing and briefing between maintenance staff, front office, and residents. Much variation in time of arrival. Some staff arrive right on time, some staff always has something else they had to do before scheduled appointments.
source_file: Surrey on the Main - Utopi D. San Francisco, CA - Apr 13, 2026.txt

id: Surrey on the Main - Utopi D. San Francisco, CA - Apr 13, 2026.txt::chunk-1
text: Beyond the initial move-in period, ~20 days for full residency, the front office once their commission and online reviews have been collected, do not make the best effort to reply to residents for potentially problematic, non-maintenance issues often citing prioritization as the reasoning for delays or non-response. Would write more, but folks think they know everything and recalcitrant about gainful change not borne from their head space. Also, some of the staff seem to have pine cones wedged very far up their a$$es, would take more than words to mitigate.
source_file: Surrey on the Main - Utopi D. San Francisco, CA - Apr 13, 2026.txt

id: The Bravern - Emily W. Portland, OR - Jun 24, 2025.txt::chunk-0
text: We enjoyed living at The Bravern for 2.5 years, but recently moved out after a proposed 17% rent increase. Unfortunately, the quality just doesn't justify the price. Elevators are often slow or out of service, the lobby lights have been broken for months, and the small gym and limited amenities (two tiny dog runs, two shared grills) are inadequate for a building this size. The concierge desk frequently closes early with the same vague "due to unforeseen circumstances" message -- it's frustrating and unprofessional. Constant nearby construction and street noise only add to the headache.
source_file: The Bravern - Emily W. Portland, OR - Jun 24, 2025.txt

id: The Bravern - Emily W. Portland, OR - Jun 24, 2025.txt::chunk-1
text: Constant nearby construction and street noise only add to the headache. For what they charge, you'd expect much better.
source_file: The Bravern - Emily W. Portland, OR - Jun 24, 2025.txt

id: The Bravern - Will M. Kirkland, WA - Dec 19, 2025.txt::chunk-0
text: I've waited to make this review until we've lived her a while. This is our first apartment - we've only been homeowners before this move. I'm a pretty optimistic person, but the Bravern has exceeded all my expectations ... it's a fabulous property. Everything ... staff, apartment finishes, appliances, security, parking, the gym, etc. ... they're all absolutely top notch. The staff really makes the difference ... Thea (concierge) and Alon (resident services manager) are great ... kind, caring and responsive. Mariano, Gene, and Shawn in maintenance are awesome ...
source_file: The Bravern - Will M. Kirkland, WA - Dec 19, 2025.txt

id: The Bravern - Will M. Kirkland, WA - Dec 19, 2025.txt::chunk-1
text: Mariano, Gene, and Shawn in maintenance are awesome ... when we've had issues they've been here within 30 minutes and resolved the problem ... in our homeowner days we would've been waiting a week to get the issue resolved. Bottom line, we couldn't be happier with our decision to move here.
source_file: The Bravern - Will M. Kirkland, WA - Dec 19, 2025.txt
